usOpenings.name: Land Collective
usOpenings.fit: Strong local target for fresher urban designers who can show public-space and site thinking.
usOpenings.summary: Pittsburgh-based practice with a visible careers page and work that overlaps landscape, public realm, and urban-scale design.

usOpenings.name: SmithGee Studio
usOpenings.fit: Useful for freshers because the studio explicitly references internships and learning exposure.
usOpenings.summary: Official internships page makes this one of the clearer Pittsburgh-area practices for emerging applicants.

usOpenings.name: Perkins Eastman
usOpenings.fit: Strong for portfolio-based fresher applications into larger urban design and planning teams.
usOpenings.summary: Large multidisciplinary practice with an official careers system and Pittsburgh presence.

usOpenings.name: Perkins&Will
usOpenings.fit: Useful for freshers because the global careers system regularly carries intern and graduate routes.
usOpenings.summary: Well-known design practice with planning and urban design exposure plus a stable official careers portal.

usOpenings.name: MKSK
usOpenings.fit: A strong nearby-state option for freshers interested in civic, campus, and mixed-use urban design work.
usOpenings.summary: Regional design studio with strong urban design and planning overlap across Ohio and adjacent markets.

usOpenings.name: WRT
usOpenings.fit: Excellent portfolio benchmark for applicants targeting urban design and landscape-led public realm work.
usOpenings.summary: Official careers page is live and the practice is directly relevant for urban design-oriented freshers.

usOpenings.name: Design Collective
usOpenings.fit: Worth targeting for fresher work because the studio blends architecture, planning, and mixed-use urban thinking.
usOpenings.summary: Mid-Atlantic option with an official careers page and urban-scale project exposure.

usOpenings.name: Toole Design
usOpenings.fit: Good if your portfolio leans toward streets, mobility, walkability, and civic design systems.
usOpenings.summary: Transport and public-realm focused practice with stable official hiring routes.

usOpenings.name: Stantec
usOpenings.fit: Useful for a broad fresher search because the site consistently carries graduate and junior routes.
usOpenings.summary: Large consultancy with recurring early-career and graduate hiring pages relevant to planning and urban design.

usOpenings.name: AECOM
usOpenings.fit: Practical fresher target if you are open to planning, urban strategy, or city-shaping consultancy roles.
usOpenings.summary: Large employer with a stable careers platform and recurring city-planning related roles.

usOpenings.name: Gensler
usOpenings.fit: Useful for freshers targeting large-scale mixed-use, strategy, and urban design teams.
usOpenings.summary: Global design practice with a stable careers portal and urban design visibility across many offices.

usOpenings.name: Sasaki
usOpenings.fit: A reach option, but highly relevant if your work leans masterplanning and interdisciplinary urban design.
usOpenings.summary: Official opportunities page stays stable and is relevant for ambitious fresher outreach.

usOpenings.name: OJB Landscape Architecture
usOpenings.fit: Useful for freshers because OJB publishes designer and intern-oriented roles across multiple offices.
usOpenings.summary: Official careers page is live and the firm has a Philadelphia presence with clear public-realm and planning relevance.

usOpenings.name: SWA Group
usOpenings.fit: Relevant for freshers with landscape-led urban design work who are open to a national studio network.
usOpenings.summary: SWA publishes openings on its official careers page and explicitly spans planning, landscape, and urban design.

usOpenings.name: HOK
usOpenings.fit: Useful for freshers because HOK explicitly describes paid student internships across planning and related disciplines.
usOpenings.summary: Official careers page highlights student internships and broad planning-oriented pathways across its studio network.

usOpenings.name: Jacobs
usOpenings.fit: Good broad employer for freshers who can position themselves across planning, transport, and city-shaping teams.
usOpenings.summary: Jacobs routes applicants through its official careers platform and publicly references graduate urban design progression in the business.

europeUkOpenings.name: Arup
europeUkOpenings.fit: Very good for freshers because Arup clearly structures graduate and early-career entry routes.
europeUkOpenings.summary: Stable official early-careers page covering graduates and emerging talent across urbanism-related teams.

europeUkOpenings.name: BDP
europeUkOpenings.fit: Strong for graduates because BDP is multidisciplinary and often suitable for planning and urbanism portfolios.
europeUkOpenings.summary: Official careers hub for a large UK design practice with planning and urban design relevance.

europeUkOpenings.name: Foster + Partners
europeUkOpenings.fit: A high-bar option, but relevant for graduates with strong urban design and masterplanning material.
europeUkOpenings.summary: Official careers portal for a globally recognised design practice with urbanism exposure.

europeUkOpenings.name: AtkinsRealis
europeUkOpenings.fit: Useful for freshers looking for a formal graduate scheme rather than studio-by-studio outreach.
europeUkOpenings.summary: Official graduate programme page and large consultancy structure make it a dependable route.

europeUkOpenings.name: Pegasus Group
europeUkOpenings.fit: Very relevant for freshers because the practice explicitly spans planning, urban design, and landscape.
europeUkOpenings.summary: Official careers route for a UK planning and urban design consultancy.

europeUkOpenings.name: Stantec UK
europeUkOpenings.fit: One of the clearest exact fresher matches because the route explicitly references graduate urban design.
europeUkOpenings.summary: Official job page for a graduate urban designer route in the UK operation.

europeUkOpenings.name: AECOM EMEA
europeUkOpenings.fit: Practical for freshers who are open to planning, strategy, and city-shaping consultancy roles across Europe.
europeUkOpenings.summary: Official careers platform covers Europe and the UK and regularly routes applicants into graduate pathways.

europeUkOpenings.name: Ramboll
europeUkOpenings.fit: Useful if your work leans sustainable cities, mobility, and strategic urban systems.
europeUkOpenings.summary: Official early-careers route for a major European consultancy with city-shaping work.

europeUkOpenings.name: Buro Happold
europeUkOpenings.fit: Relevant for freshers who want multidisciplinary city and district-scale work inside a large practice.
europeUkOpenings.summary: Official careers page for early-career applications across a broad urban systems environment.

europeUkOpenings.name: Henning Larsen
europeUkOpenings.fit: Strong studio option for freshers targeting design-led urbanism and public-space thinking.
europeUkOpenings.summary: Official listing for student or recent graduate talent entry through the studio's greenhouse board.

europeUkOpenings.name: Gustafson Porter + Bowman
europeUkOpenings.fit: Good for freshers because the studio publishes a direct recruitment address for CV and portfolio submissions.
europeUkOpenings.summary: Official join-us page invites applicants to send a CV and portfolio directly to recruitment.

europeUkOpenings.name: Arcadis
europeUkOpenings.fit: Useful for freshers who want a broad graduate intake and city-shaping consultancy exposure.
europeUkOpenings.summary: Official careers platform includes a graduates and students pathway across the wider business.

europeUkOpenings.name: HOK London
europeUkOpenings.fit: Relevant for freshers because HOK publicly notes student internships and cross-office design exposure.
europeUkOpenings.summary: Official careers page covers paid student internships and wider early-career pathways across the firm, including London.

generalDirectory.note: Worth keeping on the radar for outreach and future openings.

motivationalQuotes.quote: You do not need the whole map. You need the next strong application.
motivationalQuotes.author: Anne Lamott

motivationalQuotes.quote: Success is the sum of small efforts, repeated day in and day out.
motivationalQuotes.author: Robert Collier

motivationalQuotes.quote: The future depends on what you do today.
motivationalQuotes.author: Mahatma Gandhi

motivationalQuotes.quote: It always seems impossible until it is done.
motivationalQuotes.author: Nelson Mandela
